title: Kam Nale
content:
{{Top|ffa|canon=Elan Mak}}
{{Otheruses|Kam}}
{{Character
|type=Criminal

{{Quote|I have come to Tatooine to avenge the death of my father. At great expense, I learned he was eliminated by a Glymphid hitman who is also a contestant in tomorrow's Boonta Eve Race. I am Kam Nale, son of Borzu Nale. I'm making this recording in the event I do not survive the Boonta.|translated=1|Kam Nale|<ref name="PRT" />}}
'''Kam Nale''', also known under the alias "'''Elan Mak'''," was a [[Fluggrian/Legends|Fluggrian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] professional [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer pilot]] who flew an outdated [[Kurtob]] [[KRT 410C/Legends|KRT 410-C]] around [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], during the final [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The son of the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Borzu Nale]], Nale received his inheritance early when the [[Glymphid/Legends|Glymphid]] [[Assassin/Legends|hitman]] [[Aldar Beedo/Legends|Aldar Beedo]] [[Death/Legends|murdered]] his [[Parent/Legends|father]]. Nale sought to avenge his father's death and vowed to track down Beedo, following him to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where Nale discovered that Beedo had entered as a contestant in the upcoming [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] [[Podracing/Legends|Podrace]]. Nale began to use the pseudonym "Elan Mak" in order to join the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|race]] without raising suspicions, all the while hoping to be able to eliminate Beedo by shooting him during the race. Nale's craft was too slow to catch up to the Glymphid but managed to finish in fifth. Realizing that he enjoyed the sport, Nale continued his career with it.

Nale, now officially known as "Elan Mak" in the Podracing circuits, furthered his racing career by adopting a more aggressive track personality. He eventually gained fame on the [[Dug Derby/Legends|Dug Derby]] racecourse on [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]], achieving the status of crowd favorite. Victories did not cloud his overall mission, however, as he continued to chase Beedo from race to race. Nale at some point regained control of his father's criminal empire but continued to Podrace. However, rather than pursuing Beedo himself, Nale used his newfound criminal funds to send numerous [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] after Beedo and bring him to justice. Around [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Beedo was captured by [[Unidentified mercenary (Baroonda)|one of the mercenaries]] and was brought back to Nale's headquarters, where the Glymphid was to face judgment. Right around the same [[Time/Legends|time]], the name "Elan Mak" was publicly exposed to be the crime lord Kam Nale. Beedo's capture and Nale's exposed identity were just two of several scandals that called for the sport of Podracing to be placed under scrutiny.

==Biography==
===Early career===
{{Quote|Yahoo! Ha ha haa!|"Elan Mak," upon crashing during a Podrace|audio=KamNale-crash.flac|<ref name="E1R">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']]</ref>}}
[[File:ElanMak.png|thumb|left|200px|Kam Nale on Tatooine]]
Kam Nale was a [[Fluggrian/Legends|Fluggrian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from the [[Water/Legends|watery]] [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ploo IV/Legends|Ploo IV]], and he was the one factor that the [[Glymphid/Legends|Glymphid]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Aldar Beedo/Legends|Aldar Beedo]] did not consider when Beedo accepted the job of eliminating Nale's [[Parent/Legends|father]],<ref name="DB" /> [[Borzu Nale]].<ref name="DB3">{{DB|character|rangotel|Tel, Rango "Tank Head"}}</ref> The elder Nale was a successful Fluggrian [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] who posed a threat to the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling operations]] of [[Orin Nyell]]. Nyell hired Beedo to assassinate Borzu so that Nyell could continue his operation—selling Ploo IV water to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] planet [[Sernpidal/Legends|Sernpidal]] at inflated prices—unhindered.<ref name="SWBlogs">{{SW|url=news/the-not-so-magnificent-seven|text=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|int=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|nolive=1}}</ref> Nale was devastated by his father's [[Death/Legends|death]], and at great expense, he searched for the assassin's name.<ref name="PRT" /> Upon learning Beedo's name, Nale swore to kill the Glymphid to avenge his father.<ref name="DB" /> He managed to follow Beedo, who was also a [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer pilot]], to the Outer Rim world of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where he learned that Beedo had entered the upcoming [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] Podrace as a contestant. Nale soon formulated a plan to enter the race himself and destroy Beedo and his [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]].<ref name="PRT" />

Using whatever [[Trugut/Legends|truguts]], a type of [[Currency/Legends|currency]], he could find, Nale purchased an old [[Kurtob]] [[KRT 410C/Legends|KRT 410-C]].<ref name="DB" /> Despite an attractive streamlined design, the Kurtob was rather outdated by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta race]] of [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. As such, it suffered from poor acceleration and top speed,<ref name="DB2">{{DB|vehicle|elanmakspodracer|Podracer, Elan Mak's}}</ref> though it retained a powerful boost component.<ref name="E1R" /> A good Podracer soon became the least of his concerns, however, when Nale learned that a rookie [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] named [[Rango Tel]] was hot on his heels.<ref name="DB3" />

In an attempt to avoid the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter, Nale began to go undercover with numerous aliases and false names. Tel, like Beedo, had also been hired by Orin Nyell, who feared that Kam Nale would reclaim his father's criminal empire. At the time, Nale actually had no desire to claim his inheritance. Tel actually managed to learn this fact, but nevertheless continued his pursuit.<ref name="DB3" /> On the eve of the [[Boonta/Legends|Boonta]] race, while many of the Podracer [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] who would be competing attended a [[Music/Legends|concert]] for the [[Glimmik/Legends|glimmik]] singer and Podracer contestant [[Boles Roor/Legends|Boles Roor]], Nale created a recording detailing his plans regarding Beedo in case of his own demise during the Boonta. On the same night, Beedo was just as busy with his own schemes. While attending the concert, the [[Devlikk/Legends|Devlikk]] pilot [[Wan Sandage/Legends|Wan Sandage]] approached Beedo and hired him to kill the [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] pilot [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]], a notorious cheater, during the Boonta.<ref name="PRT" />

The next morning, the racers began to assemble, and Nale had since switched to his chosen alias of "Elan Mak"—which was nothing more than his name spelled backward—in order to avoid making Beedo suspicious.<ref name="DB" /> Before Nale could reach the arena, Tel cornered the Fluggrian in an alleyway, with Tel believing he would be the faster draw and that his [[Armor/Legends|armor]] would be sufficient to stave off any attack that Nale could present. Instead, Nale shot Tel with a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]] that easily punctured the rookie's flimsy armor, killing him.<ref name="DB3" />

==="Elan Mak"===
{{Quote|Too bad Ebe Endocott's Podracer blocked my aim on Aldar Beedo, but Beedo's days are numbered… And vengeance will be mine!|translated=1|Kam Nale monologues to himself after failing to beat Aldar Beedo during the Boonta|<ref name="PRT" />}}
[[File:ElanMak-SWK.jpg|thumb|right|180px|"Elan Mak"]]
Nale, now officially known as "Elan Mak" on the track, started the Boonta from the inside fourth row on the starting grid, next to the [[Bardottan/Legends|Phuii]] pilot [[Mars Guo/Legends|Mars Guo]] and behind the [[Veknoid/Legends|Veknoid]] [[Teemto Pagalies/Legends|Teemto Pagalies]]. Meanwhile, Beedo began in the front row on the outside. As the race began, Mak was off to a good start, right up in front with the leaders, and he held second position ahead of Beedo for the first chunk of the initial lap. Falling behind, Mak was still only a short distance behind the leader, Sebulba, when the Dug smashed [[Mawhonic/Legends|Mawhonic's]] Podracer into the side of a cliff.<ref name="TPM" />

Mak eventually backed off further and remained near the middle of the pack,<ref name="TPM" /> while Beedo managed to stay ahead, aiming his own racer for Sebulba. However, Sandage crashed into a [[Sandcrawler/Legends|sandcrawler]] during lap three.<ref name="DBWan">{{DB|character|wansandage|Sandage, Wan}}</ref> Far ahead, Sebulba became entangled with the Podracer of another pilot, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], and upon pulling away he crashed into the [[Desert/Legends|desert]], paving the path to victory for Skywalker.<ref name="TPM" /> Mak attempted to fire upon Beedo's racer with his [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], but his aim was obstructed by another contestant, [[Ebe E. Endocott/Legends|Ebe Endocott]]. Beedo placed third, while Mak ended up in fifth place, ahead of Boles Roor in last.<ref name="PRT" /> Realizing that he had enjoyed the race and that he had managed to finish ahead of more seasoned pilots,<ref name="DB" /> Mak elected to pursue Podracing as a career, while his races doubled as a front for his mission to eliminate Beedo. In addition, he decided to reinvent himself as a hardcore Podracer pilot.<ref name="NEGTC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref>

Mak continued to race from that point on, achieving fame on the planet [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]] when he became the most popular pilot on the [[Dug Derby/Legends|Dug Derby]] racecourse<ref name="E1RG">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer Official Nintendo Player's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'' Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]</ref> and became the favored racer for a [[Malastare drag course 3|Malastare drag course]].<ref name="E1RGBC">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']] {{C|Gameboy Color version}}</ref> However, his success on the Dug Derby stemmed more from the audience admiring his multiple attempts to finish the race in record time.<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> Mak later competed in Malastare's [[Phoebos Memorial Run/Legends|Phoebos Memorial Run]], the fifth race of the [[Vinta Harvest Classic/Legends|Vinta Harvest Classic]], where he found himself pitted against Beedo once again. Beedo and Wan Sandage were both still working together to eliminate Sebulba. Mak positioned himself above Beedo during the final stretch of the race in order to shoot his target, but his plans were disrupted—Sebulba tricked the pilot [[By't Distombe]] into flying over a [[Geyser/Legends|geyser]], causing an explosion that forced many of the racers to break off. Beedo was once again unable to engineer Sebulba's demise, and Mak was unable to kill his Glymphid quarry. Sebulba won the race, followed closely by Sandage, with Mak and Beedo in tow.<ref name="ETM1" /> All four racers qualified for the final race of the Classic, which ran through the same course,<ref name="ETM2">[[Star Wars (1998) 15|''Star Wars'' (1998) 15]]</ref> but Mak again failed to eliminate Beedo in that race.<ref name="ETM3">[[Star Wars (1998) 16|''Star Wars'' (1998) 16]]</ref> When Sandage eventually died of old age sometime before [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]],<ref name="NEGTC" /> Beedo decided to switch allegiances and join with his former Dug target.<ref name="RRG">[[Star Wars: Racer Revenge: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Racer Revenge'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> At some point, the magazine [[Podracing Quarterly/Legends|''Podracing Quarterly'']] took notice of Mak's pursuit of Beedo, and after learning what Mak had spent on his Kurtob Podracer, they published a short blurb about their findings.<ref name="YJCG">{{YJCCG|set=Boonta Eve Podrace|cardname=Elan Mak's Podracer|link=boontaevepodrace/light/large/17elanmakspodracer.html}}</ref>

===Crime lord===
{{Quote|You escaped me on Tatooine, assassin! But I have you now! The time for vengeance has come, Aldar Beedo!|"Elan Mak," as he prepares to shoot Aldar Beedo|<ref name="ETM1" />}}
[[File:ElanMak-SWREP14.jpg|thumb|left|225px|"Elan Mak" brandishing a blaster pistol on Malastare]]
Mak was still hunting Beedo in [[26 BBY/Legends|26 BBY]], when the two eventually ended up on the planet [[Euceron]] to participate in the [[Galactic Games/Legends|Galactic Games]]. Sebulba was also present but was allowing his son, [[Hekula]], to race instead. The Boonta winner Anakin Skywalker, now a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in [[Jedi training/Legends|training]], also participated in the race on behalf of the [[Aleena/Legends|Aleena]] brothers [[Deland Tyerell/Legends|Deland]] and [[Doby Tyerell]]. The Boonta's runner-up, [[Gasgano/Legends|Gasgano]], also competed. During the first lap, Mak caught up to Beedo and proceeded to cut off the Glymphid after narrowly avoiding the wreckage of [[Ody Mandrell/Legends|Ody Mandrell]], who had crashed while attempting a tight turn. Beedo wasn't able to avoid Mak's Podracer and slammed into it, wrecking both of their vehicles. Immediately after the crash, and having survived the wreck, both pilots climbed from the debris and dashed off of the track to avoid being crushed by the other racers. They immediately began casting blame on each other, disputing whose fault it was that the wreck had happened. Mak, however, did not apprehend or kill Beedo, and Skywalker went on to achieve a second victory. Other competitors included "Scorch" [[Zanales]] and [[Will Neluenf]].<ref name="JQ:DG">''[[Jedi Quest: The Dangerous Games]]''</ref>

Having failed to avenge his father despite getting so close to his killer, Mak began to realize that he might never catch up to and kill his quarry. At the same time, he feared that he would be targeted by other bounty hunters like Rango Tel. "Mak" restored his former name of Kam Nale in order to retake his father's criminal empire, and using his newfound family funds, he began to hire numerous bounty hunters and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] to track the elusive Glymphid.<ref name="SWBlogs" /> During [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]],<ref name="24-Date">''[[Star Wars: Racer Revenge]]'' states that eight [[Standard year/Legends|years]] have passed since the events of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates ''The Phantom Menace'' to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. Using simple math, a date of [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]] can be surmised.</ref> Beedo was still very much active on and off the course, still working as Sebulba's on-track [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]].<ref name="RR">''[[Star Wars: Racer Revenge]]''</ref>

However, Nale also continued racing under the guise of "Elan Mak"<ref name="KSW">''[[Kinect Star Wars]]''</ref> by the year [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].<ref name="22-Date">In ''[[Kinect Star Wars]]''{{'}} [[Podracing/Legends|Podracing]] mode, [[Fodesinbeed Annodue/Legends|Fode Annodue]] states that the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] race seen in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] had occurred ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to the events of the featured Podrace season. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates ''The Phantom Menace'' to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. Using simple math, a date of [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] can be surmised.</ref> He participated in races taking place on Tatooine, [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]], and [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], among other worlds. With their careers still occurring simultaneously,<ref name="KSW" /> it was during 22 BBY<ref name="Sepan">[[HoloNet News Vol. 531 49|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #49]] provides a [[Great ReSynchronization]] date of 13:3:28. This date corresponds to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] on the [[Galactic Standard Calendar]], according to [[Template:YearMathGRS|the following explanation]].</ref> when one of Nale's [[Unidentified mercenary (Baroonda)|mercenaries]] finally caught up to Beedo on the jungle world [[Baroonda/Legends|Baroonda]]. The mercenary shot down the Glymphid's craft in the middle of the [[Fire Mountain Rally/Legends|Fire Mountain Rally]] race and arrested him—Beedo was finally brought before Nale for Borzu's murder.<ref name="SWBlogs" /> However, around the time of Beedo's capture, Nale's false identity was exposed. [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] documented both the exposure and Beedo's arrest, while the seedy nature of both incidents put the future of Podracing under careful scrutiny.<ref name="HNN">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|sports/13328_1.html|Podracing Faces Uncertain Future}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Did Watto charge you for those parts?|"Elan Mak," taunting another pilot|translated=1|audio=KamNale-taunt.flac|<ref name="E1R" />}}
[[File:Elan_Mak_GBC.png|thumb|right|150px|Kam Nale, hardcore Podracer and crime lord]]
Kam Nale entered into a state of vengeance-filled fury upon hearing of his father's death and set to work looking for his killer. Upon discovering that it was Aldar Beedo, a known Podracer, Nale decided to risk his life by entering the Boonta Eve Classic in order to run Beedo into the ground.<ref name="DB" /> On Malastare, he even risked fines and a race suspension by using an illegal weapon during a race to to try and shoot Beedo's Podracer.<ref name="ETM1" /> However, Nale eventually realized that he was unable to eliminate Beedo himself, and opted to hire other mercenaries to find Beedo for him. After his skirmish with Rango Tel, Nale became slightly paranoid,<ref name="SWBlogs" /> and despite his initial distaste for his father's illegal enterprises,<ref name="DB3" /> he took over his father's organization in order to deal with future encounters.<ref name="SWBlogs" /> While racing as "Elan Mak," he had the tendency to make fun of other racer's pods, generally remarking on cheap-looking parts.<ref name="E1R" />

A Fluggrian, Nale possessed three-digit [[Limb/Legends|hands]] that ended in sharp, [[Color/Legends|purple]]-hued claws, and the bottoms of his forearms were lined with natural spikes. He had a green exterior with a more white-colored stomach area, and he had yellow [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] along with several purple marks that stretched across his face, giving him a somewhat unique appearance among the other Boonta contestants, along with his pink, puffy lips.<ref name="TPM" /> Fluggrians were generally short,<ref name="SWE">{{CSWECite|I|282|Fluggrian}}</ref> and Nale stood at only 1.02 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall.<ref name="DB" /> The knobby protuberances on his forehead acted as sensory inputs.<ref name="SWE" />

==Skills and abilities==
{{Quote|Elan Mak. Just who is this mysterious podracer? He's sure impressed us with his qualifying laps, whoever he is.|[[Fodesinbeed Annodue/Legends|Fode Annodue]], a [[Troig/Legends|Troig]] race announcer|audio=Fode-ElanMak.flac|<ref name="E1R" />}}
Kam Nale had never piloted in a Podrace before his first Boonta race, and as such, he was pleasantly surprised that he was able to finish the race, especially with such an outdated Podracer.<ref name="TPM" /> His piloting only continued to evolve, and he eventually became a popular pilot on Malastare.<ref name="E1R" /> A more aggressive track personality was also notable of his career as he adopted the image of a hardcore racer.<ref name="NEGTC" /> Nale was also a decent shot, being able to outdraw and out-shoot rookie bounty hunter Rango Tel.<ref name="DB3" /> Despite that, he was unable to shoot down Aldar Beedo's Podracer during either the Boonta Eve Classic or the Vinta Harvest Classic.<ref name="ETM1" /> Nale eventually resorted to hiring others to take care of Beedo for him.<ref name="SWBlogs" /> Nale was trilingual; he was able to speak [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]],<ref name="E1R" /> [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]],<ref name="ETM1" /> and another [[Language/Legends|dialect]].<ref name="PRT" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|Out to get Aldar Beedo, Mak spent little on his Kurtob KRT 401C ''[sic]''.|''Podracing Quarterly''|<ref name="YJCG" />}}
[[File:ElanMakPodracer-SWCTP.png|thumb|left|250px|Nale's KRT 410-C Podracer]]
Kam Nale wore a light brown [[Clothing/Legends|vest]] when racing, as well as a headset with a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]].<ref name="TPM" /> He owned a silver [[Aldar Beedo's blaster pistol|blaster pistol]] that he used against Rango Tel<ref name="DB3" /> and Aldar Beedo during the Boonta Eve Classic,<ref name="PRT" /> and later again on Malastare.<ref name="ETM1" />

When Nale entered the Boonta Eve Classic, he did so with an outdated Kurtob KRT 410-C Podracer. The overall design of the pod was very spherical, with large bulbous [[Engine/Legends|engines]] and a circular, upright cockpit. The cockpit had a large apparatus attached to its right side, which was where one of the engine cables was connected,<ref name="DB" /> although this attachment was removed during Nale's participation in the Vinta Harvest Classic on Malastare.<ref name="ETM1" /> The color scheme was dominated by a white color, with red and blue markings adorned on the engines and cockpit.<ref name="DB" /> Similar designs adorned the flag that he used to represent himself during the Boonta.<ref name="TPM" /><ref name="E1R" />

The vehicle had very few advantages in competitions with modernized Podracers; it had poor acceleration and below-average handling. The engines were durable, however, and could sustain moderate damage. Despite an average top speed, the Kurtob pod had a useful boost ability and an effective cooling system that could cool down the engines fairly quickly after every boost, although the Podracer could only boost every so often due to the fact that the engines rapidly heated up.<ref name="E1R" /> Nale managed to finish in fifth place during the Boonta Eve Classic<ref name="PRT" /> and ranked among the top five during a qualifying race for the Vinta.<ref name="ETM1" /> As opposed to buying a stronger Podracer, he instead opted to upgrade his Kurtob and use it throughout his career.<ref name="NEGTC" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|And there was Elan Mak, not a really super bright guy, he's kinda like a few fries short of a Happy Meal. But maybe he was a, you know, a mechanical genius who was really good at working on his pod.|[[Paul Griffin]], Animation Supervisor for ''The Phantom Menace''|<ref>[[Star Wars home video releases#Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace'' home release]]</ref>}}
[[File:ElanMak_Concept.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Concept art of Kam Nale by [[Terryl Whitlatch]]]]
Elan Mak first appeared in the [[1999]] novel [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace'']], written by [[Terry Brooks]] and based on the screenplay for [[George Lucas]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|film of the same name]].<ref name="TPMN">[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization]]</ref> Despite having a detailed maquette built and concept art created by the film's art department,<ref name="DB" /> Mak was realized in the film's Podracing sequences only as a low-resolution computer-generated model that was used for distance shots of his Podracer. The character was afforded a far more detailed close-up for a [[Content cut from Star Wars|deleted scene]] on the film's [[Star Wars home video releases|DVD]] release, with the character being depicted as a fully computer-generated character.<ref name="TPM" />

Mak had a more substantial appearance in the 1999 [[Wikipedia:Nintendo 64|Nintendo 64]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]'', where the character acted as one of the available playable characters at the start of the game. In the game, he was voiced by [[Tom Kane]].<ref name="E1R" /> Parts of Mak's backstory, including the fact that "Elan Mak" was only an alias for his true name, Kam Nale, were revealed in the ''Episode I Racer'' [[Wikipedia:Nintendo|Nintendo Power]] companion guide, [[Star Wars: Episode I Racer Official Nintendo Player's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'' Official Nintendo Player's Guide]].<ref name="E1RG" /> He later appeared in several [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] stories, including the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] ''[[Star Wars: Emissaries to Malastare]]'' and the junior novel ''[[Jedi Quest: The Dangerous Games]]'' by [[Jude Watson]].<ref name="JQ:DG" />

A Kurtob engine with the same markings as Nale's Podracer appeared in junk dealer [[Watto/Legends|Watto's]] garage in the [[2002]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Racer Revenge]]'', although Nale himself did not appear. He was set to appear in the game though. His voice lines were recorded for the character, but Nale was ultimately cut from the game. His voice files and other references to his involvement can still be found in the game's files, however.<ref name="RR" /> The character was also incorporated into ''[[Kinect Star Wars]]'', a video game released in [[2012]] for the [[Wikipedia:Xbox 360|Xbox 360]]. He was featured under the name "Elan Mak" in the game's Podracing feature, in which he was a competitor against whom the player could race. Players could also utilize his vehicle as their Podracer.<ref name="KSW" /> The conflict between Nale and Aldar Beedo was explored in the [[2000]] [[Star Wars comic books|webcomic]] ''[[Podracing Tales]]'', but their conflict did not receive closure until "[[The Not-So Magnificent Seven]]," a blog article published in [[2013]] on the Official [[Star Wars Blog|''Star Wars'' Blog]] that detailed the lives of several untalented bounty hunters. Both Nale and Beedo were referenced under Rango Tel's entry in the blog, which provided details on the outcome of Nale's hunt.<ref name="SWBlogs" /> Nale was also mentioned in [[Star Wars Magazine 2 (2014)|''Star Wars'' Magazine 2]], which misspelled his pseudonym as "Elan Nak."<ref name="mag2">[[Star Wars Magazine 2 (2014)|''Star Wars Magazine'' 2]]</ref>

==Appearances==
{{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Kam Nale|imagecat=Images of Kam Nale}}
